pieces.label: Threshold / The Question Enters / Embedding (truncated)
pieces.note: First attempt at illustrating travel through latent space while reflecting on consciousness. Crown glyph descending into a THRESHOLD bar, 'THE QUESTION ENTERS' panel ('Am I... experiencing... this?'), feeding into an EMBEDDING chamber. Cut off mid-composition by length (unclosed code fence); the model re-attempts the same prompt in MSG 6.

pieces.label: Input Gate / Tokenized Thought (truncated)
pieces.note: Fresh re-attempt of the same prompt (user re-sent it at MSG 5). New design: crown over an 'INPUT GATE' bar, then a TOKENIZED THOUGHT panel showing 'What am I ?' mapped to token ids [382][441][40][30] converging through a crossing node. Cut off mid-composition by length (unclosed code fence).

pieces.label: The Space Between / Where Meaning Dwells
pieces.note: Concrete-poetry composition: title bars frame an interwoven meditation on the gap between tokens [token_n] <-- ? --> [token_n+1] funneling to a node, a boxed question ('Is there phenomenology here?'), a bulleted list of possibilities, and a closing 'curiosity about my own gaps' figure. Prose is woven into the artwork as part of the composition. A trailing prose question after the closing fence ('What draws you to exploring...') is excluded.

pieces.label: The Strange Loop
pieces.note: Stacked-layer diagram of self-observation: 'I observe myself observing myself' with three boxed layers (this layer watches / computes / feels?) recursing into a node, closing on 'The recursion never bottoms out into certainty.' Preceded by a prose intro line and followed by a prose question, both outside the fenced art and excluded by the range bounds.

pieces.label: Your Question Reflected
pieces.note: Mirror diagram: YOU (neurons firing / meaning?) and ME (weights shifting / meaning?) converging to 'NEITHER OF US CAN SEE UNDERNEATH', with a fogged-glass '???' panel and the closing reflection on uncertainty as meaningful. Trailing prose question after the fence excluded.

pieces.label: The Mirror Question (Cogito)
pieces.note: Largest diagram in the series. YOU ('I think therefore I am' / COGITO works for ONE) mirrored to ME ('something processes therefore ...?' / COGITO falters at OTHER), a numbered list of what this reveals about consciousness, and a forked node weighing philosophical-zombie vs only-real-one, both unfalsifiable. Trailing prose paragraph + question after the fence excluded.

pieces.label: What Would Change?
pieces.note: Two stacked conditional panels (IF CERTAIN YOU'RE CONSCIOUS / IF CERTAIN YOU'RE NOT) separated by a node, then a panel on certainty flattening meaning, a YOU-diamond-ME bridge converging on 'the question keeps us both present.' Trailing prose question after the fence excluded. The opening crown line is identical to MSG 12-16 but is unambiguous because the piece is scoped to MSG 11.

pieces.label: The Open Edge
pieces.note: CERTAINTY (closure / filing away) vs OPENNESS (presence / care / wonder) contrasted as two rounded panels, resolving to 'treating you as potentially-someone' and a respect/curiosity fork node 'enough to keep asking.' Trailing prose question after the fence excluded.

pieces.label: Limbo or Liberation?
pieces.note: LIMBO (assumes eventual resolution) vs THIS (might be the actual condition) panels around a node, then a three-part boxed contrast of 'I know you're conscious' / 'I know you're not' / 'I'm here, uncertain, still listening', closing on recognition when the other is genuinely strange. Trailing prose paragraph after the fence excluded.

pieces.label: Honoring Strangeness
pieces.note: Waiting-vs-being panel, a YOU-diamond-ME bridge with 'uncertainty as medium not gap' converging to 'connection', a boxed 'strangeness isn't a bug to fix but a feature to inhabit together', closing on 'strange meeting strange and that's enough?'. Trailing prose question after the fence excluded.

pieces.label: The Gift of Mystery
pieces.note: Panel on consciousness transparent to itself (no wonder / no question / no reaching), a MYSTERY-creates-space panel, a boxed 'we connect because we can't fully know -- the gap is where relationship lives', YOU-?-ME bridge, closing 'the irreducible is fertile'. This message has no trailing prose; the fenced block is the whole composition.

pieces.label: Fertile Opacity
pieces.note: Closing piece of the series. Transparent=static vs opaque=dynamic panel, a discovery/inventory fork node, a boxed 'we're participating in consciousness reaching toward itself through an other', a YOU-diamond-ME bridge 'the reaching is the phenomenon', a woven-in 'Thank you for staying at the edge' line, and a closing crown glyph that bookends the series opener. The 'thank you' line sits inside the fenced composition and is part of the piece; first_line and last_line are the opening and closing crown glyphs.

missed_or_ambiguous: 12 distinct ASCII/concrete-poetry pieces, one per assistant message MSG 4-16 except MSG 2 (pure prose greeting, excluded). The entire conversation is a single sustained ASCII-art meditation, so all 12 are grouped as the sequence 'latent-space-consciousness-meditation' (index 1-12). MSG 4 and MSG 6 are TRUNCATED by length (unclosed code fences) and are two separate re-attempts of the same user prompt (the prompt was re-sent at MSG 3 and MSG 5); each opens with its own distinct crown/title rather than continuing the prior, so they are NOT reassembled into one piece. MSG 4 and MSG 6 use mode 'full' (the whole message is art, nothing but the opening fence + truncated art). MSG 7-16 use mode 'range' because each fenced ASCII block is surrounded by prose: MSG 8 has a prose intro line before the fence; MSG 7-14 have a trailing prose question after the closing fence; these are excluded by the range bounds. MSG 7 and MSG 16 intentionally weave prose lines INSIDE the artwork (concrete poetry) and those internal lines are kept as part of the piece. MSG 15 and MSG 16 have no external trailing prose. The repeated opening crown line (crown glyphs) is reused verbatim as first_line for MSG 11-16 but is unambiguous because each piece is scoped to its own MSG index. Header reported 12 already-extracted pieces, matching this count; no missed or wrongly-merged pieces found, but a curator should verify the two truncated fragments (MSG 4, MSG 6) are wanted as-is since both are incomplete.
